name: Childhood Autism Rating Scale
description: 15 items rated 1–4 each. Below 30 = non-autistic; 30–36 = mild-moderate; 37–60 = severe autism. A drop of ≥5 points is considered clinically meaningful.

name: Autism Treatment Evaluation Checklist
description: 4 subscales: Speech/Language/Communication (0–28), Sociability (0–40), Sensory/Cognitive Awareness (0–36), Health/Physical/Behavior (0–75). Lower total = better outcomes.

name: Aberrant Behavior Checklist
description: 5 subscales: Irritability, Lethargy, Stereotypy, Hyperactivity, Inappropriate Speech. Lower scores indicate improved behavior.

name: Clinical Global Impression – Improvement
description: 1=Very much improved, 2=Much improved, 3=Minimally improved, 4=No change, 5–7=Worse. Scores of 1–2 are considered clinically significant improvement.

name: Vineland Adaptive Behavior Scales
description: Measures Communication, Daily Living Skills, Socialization, Motor Skills. Standard scores with mean=100, SD=15. Higher = better adaptive functioning.
